2009-09-01.title: An Italian Road That May Be Too Scenic
2009-09-01.abstract: As Italy moves to replace traditional intersections with roundabouts for safety reasons, there are worries that artwork at the traffic circles could distract drivers.

2009-09-01.title: Changes Proposed to Benefit State’s Oil Giant in Brazil
2009-09-01.abstract: The country’s new regulatory framework represents a nationalistic shift for Brazil, which has discovered deep-sea fields in the past two years.

2009-09-01.title: Turkey: No Kurd Autonomy or Amnesty, Official Says
2009-09-01.abstract: A government plan to reach a resolution on ending violence by Kurdish separatists will not include autonomy for Kurds or an amnesty for the rebels.

2009-09-01.title: Arms Supplier Admits Guilt in Pentagon Contract
2009-09-01.abstract: An arms dealer who supplied ordnance for Afghanistan’s security forces admitted that he had arranged the repackaging of old cartridges for assault rifles.

2009-09-01.title: Japan’s Victors Set to Abandon Market Reform
2009-09-01.abstract: Japan’s opposition party returned to power on a pledge to a shift away from deregulation and market-led growth.

2009-09-02.title: U.N. Reports on Developing Nations’ Energy Needs
2009-09-02.abstract: Trillions of dollars are needed to help developing nations grow using renewable energy, according to an estimate.

2009-09-02.title: C.I.A. Resists Disclosure of Records on Detention
2009-09-02.abstract: The Central Intelligence Agency is refusing to make public internal documents about the agency’s now defunct detention and interrogation program.

2009-09-02.title: Misconduct Claimed at U.S. Embassy in Kabul
2009-09-02.abstract: Accounts say that security has been seriously compromised by mismanagement and misbehavior among guards and their supervisors.

2009-09-02.title: Status of the Afghan Vote Count
2009-09-02.abstract: Maps showing the percentage of polling stations that have been tallied so far and vote totals for the leading candidates in each province.

2009-09-02.title: Taiwan: Former First Lady Sentenced
2009-09-02.abstract: Wu Shu-Chen was sentenced by a Taipei court on Tuesday to a year in prison for abetting false testimony in the corruption case against her and her family.

2009-09-03.title: New U.S. Ambassador to China Predicts Broad Engagement
2009-09-03.abstract: BEIJING — The new United States ambassador to China, Jon M. Huntsman Jr., laid out a vision of close engagement with China on a broad range of issues on Wednesday, including regional stability and the environment, and said that human rights must continue to be a major part of bilateral talks.

2009-09-03.title: U.N. Guide for Sex Ed Generates Opposition
2009-09-03.abstract: A set of proposed international sex education guidelines by Unesco, aimed at reducing H.I.V. infections among young people, has provoked criticism from conservatives.

2009-09-03.title: Greece: Premier Calls for Elections, Seeking ‘a Fresh Public Mandate’
2009-09-03.abstract: Prime Minister Kostas Karamanlis is struggling with a sputtering economy, allegations of cronyism and widespread outrage over his government’s handling of last month’s omnivorous forest fires.

2009-09-03.title: Ecuador: Inquiry on Bribe Accusations
2009-09-03.abstract: Evidence obtained by Chevron seemed to indicate a $3 million bribery scheme that the company said involved representatives of the political party of President Rafael Correa and the judge overseeing a $27 billion contamination lawsuit against Chevron.

2009-09-03.title: Canada: Ex-Prosecutor Denies Guilt in the Death of a Bicycle Courier
2009-09-03.abstract: Michael Bryant, the former attorney general of Ontario, said on Wednesday that he was not guilty of criminal charges related to the death of a bicycle courier in downtown Toronto.

2009-09-04.title: N. Korea Reports Advances in Enriching Uranium
2009-09-04.abstract: North Korea’s statement would appear to end a debate within American intelligence agencies about whether the country was working on a second pathway to building nuclear weapons.

2009-09-04.title: Greek Premier, Dogged by Many Troubles, Takes Risk With Snap Elections
2009-09-04.abstract: Elections were not due before September 2011, and Kostas Karamanlis had consistently said he would serve out his four-year term.

2009-09-04.title: China Fails to Prevent Myanmar’s Ethnic Clashes
2009-09-04.abstract: China has remained close to Myanmar’s ruling junta but also to ethnic groups that are in conflict with the country’s leaders.

2009-09-04.title: Corrections
2009-09-04.abstract: A picture caption on Tuesday with an article about a debate in France over whether the government should ban the burqa, the term commonly used to describe the head-covering cloak worn by many devout Muslim women, misstated the date of the photograph showing veiled Muslim women marching in Paris. It was taken in 1994, during a protest against a French ban on Muslim head scarves in public schools — not this year.

2009-09-04.title: JetBlue Plane Is Evacuated in Bahamas
2009-09-04.abstract: As the plane was touching down in Nassau, flames were spotted near the lefthand engine. All 93 on board escaped safely.

2009-09-05.title: Under Pressure, Ecuadorean Judge Steps Aside in Suit Against Chevron
2009-09-05.abstract: The judge, Juan Núñez, whose name was mentioned in recordings as a possible recipient in the bribery scheme, said he had handed the case over to his surrogate judge.

2009-09-05.title: Gates Assails News Agency for Publishing Photo of Marine Killed in Afghanistan
2009-09-05.abstract: The father of the dead Marine had asked The Associated Press that the image of his son not be shown, saying it would hurt the family.

2009-09-05.title: With Sharp Satire, Enfant Terrible Challenges Czech Identity
2009-09-05.abstract: David Cerny, the Czech bad boy of the eastern European art world, is pursuing an artistic and political rebellion that is bolder and louder than his predecessors’.

2009-09-05.title: Drought Puts Focus on a Side of India Left Out of Progress
2009-09-05.abstract: The crisis has underscored the problems facing Indian agriculture as the population expands at the same time that water resources come under greater pressure.

2009-09-05.title: Italy: Premier Scolds Press
2009-09-05.abstract: Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi criticized the press for its coverage of the resignation of a newspaper editor after he was attacked by a newspaper owned by Mr. Berlusconi’s brother.

2009-09-06.title: Corrections
2009-09-06.abstract: An article last Sunday about secret documents obtained by the American Civil Liberties Union on the abuse of prisoners in American custody misstated, in some editions, the name of a New Jersey law firm that had worked with the A.C.L.U. and other advocacy groups on the case. It is Gibbons P.C., not Gibson P.C.

2009-09-06.title: Hundreds Saved From Philippine Ferry Sinking
2009-09-06.abstract: More than 900 people were rescued when a ferry with 1,000 on board sank in the Philippines early Sunday. At least nine died and 33 were missing.

2009-09-06.title: Mexican Candidate and Family Are Killed in Apparent Drug Hit
2009-09-06.abstract: The authorities believe a state legislative candidate in Tabasco, José Francisco Fuentes Esperón, his wife and two children were murdered by drug traffickers.

2009-09-06.title: Sudan Court to Define Indecent Dress for Women
2009-09-06.abstract: The trial of a journalist arrested for wearing pants is to begin Monday in Sudan, part of which is governed under an Islamic code that emphasizes modest dress for women.

2009-09-06.title: North Korea Opens Dam Flow, Sweeping Away 6 in the South
2009-09-06.abstract: North Korea unleashed walls of water from one of its dams Sunday, sending a flash flood roaring through the border with South Korea, killing six South Koreans.

2009-09-07.title: Ex-President Denounces Iran’s Government
2009-09-07.abstract: Mohammad Khatami made a fiery speech accusing Iran’s leaders of trying to smear their enemies and purge them from public life with “fascist and totalitarian methods.”

2009-09-07.title: Private Motive for Egypt’s Public Embrace of a Jewish Past
2009-09-07.abstract: The culture minister’s desire to be the next director general of Unesco may have helped push a suddenly public display of affection for the country’s Jewish past.

2009-09-07.title: Fake Afghan Poll Sites Favored Karzai, Officials Assert
2009-09-07.abstract: Election workers loyal to President Hamid Karzai set up hundreds of fictitious polling sites that delivered votes for the incumbent, according to officials.

2009-09-07.title: Hundreds Saved From Philippine Ferry Sinking
2009-09-07.abstract: Only two passengers remained missing Monday afternoon after nearly 1,000 were rescued following the sinking of a ferry in the southern Philippines, transportation officials said.

2009-09-07.title: Aid Agency Says U.S. Soldiers Raided an Afghan Hospital
2009-09-07.abstract: A Swedish aid agency said that U.S. soldiers stormed through one of its hospitals last week, searching for wounded Taliban fighters, breaking down doors, and tying up hospital staffers and visitors.

2009-09-08.title: Pakistan: Drone Strike Kills 5 Militants
2009-09-08.abstract: A missile believed to have been fired from an American drone struck a compound in the village of Machi Khel.

2009-09-08.title: Mine Explosion in China Kills Dozens
2009-09-08.abstract: A gas explosion at an unlicensed mine in central China killed at least 35 workers and trapped 44 more, the national work safety agency said Tuesday.

2009-09-08.title: History Lessons and Counterinsurgency
2009-09-08.abstract: Is it possible we will have lost the war at home, even if we are capable, unlike others who have tried in Afghanistan, to win it on the battlefield?

2009-09-08.title: Gas Explosion Kills 35 Miners and Traps 44, China Says
2009-09-08.abstract: Days after one of China’s senior leaders proclaimed mine safety to be a top priority, a gas explosion at an unlicensed mine in central China killed at least 35 workers and trapped 44.

2009-09-08.title: South Korea Demands Apology From North Over Dam Incident
2009-09-08.abstract: Unsatisfied with North Korea’s explanation for its release of dam water that created a flash flood in the South that killed at least three people, South Korea demanded a formal apology from the North.

2009-09-09.title: A Country of Records
2009-09-09.abstract: If Guinness World Records ever creates a category for the country most obsessed with being in the Guinness book of world records, Mexico will surely be in the running.

2009-09-09.title: Marred Afghan Vote Leaves U.S. in a Delicate Spot
2009-09-09.abstract: Concerns over vote fraud are conflicting with an interest in maintaining relations with President Hamid Karzai.

2009-09-09.title: Judge Says Trial of Bosnian Serb Leader Must Start in October
2009-09-09.abstract: The war crimes tribunal in The Hague said the trial of Radovan Karadzic, the former Bosnian Serb leader, should start Oct. 19 and conclude within 30 months.

2009-09-09.title: Georgia: Captain of Turkish Ship to Be Released
2009-09-09.abstract: The captain of a Turkish cargo ship was sentenced last week to 24 years in prison for trying to deliver fuel to a break-away Georgian enclave, Abkhazia.

2009-09-09.title: Leashing the Blogs of War
2009-09-09.abstract: The top brass can Tweet but there’s less tolerance for blogging from the field.

2009-09-10.title: Child Mortality Rate Declines Globally
2009-09-10.abstract: The child mortality rate has declined by more than a quarter in the last two decades partly because of the widening distribution of relatively inexpensive technologies.

2009-09-10.title: From Deep Pacific, Ugly and Tasty, With a Catch
2009-09-10.abstract: A deepwater Pacific fish used in fast-food sandwiches and sushi has touched off arguments over the sustainability of the catch.

2009-09-10.title: U.S. Stance Toward Russia Again Divides Europe
2009-09-10.abstract: An international survey showed that Europeans are divided over the role of the United States and how to respond to the growing assertiveness of Russia.

2009-09-10.title: Sultan Munadi: A Gentle Stalwart
2009-09-10.abstract: The Afghan reporter, who died during a rescue by British commandos on Wednesday, worked for the Kabul bureau of The New York Times.

2009-09-10.title: Correction
2009-09-10.abstract: An article on Friday about analysts who said that China has had difficulty dissuading leaders of neighboring Myanmar from renewing fighting with rebels there misstated the timing and the site of a reported meeting between officials from the two countries. It is believed to have taken place in August, not July, and it was in Myanmar, not China.

2009-09-11.title: German Geothermal Project Leads to Second Thoughts After the Earth Rumbles
2009-09-11.abstract: German officials are reviewing the safety of a plant that extracts heat from below the earth’s surface, an operation that scientists say set off an earthquake last month.

2009-09-11.title: Obama Faces Doubts From Democrats on Afghanistan
2009-09-11.abstract: A key Senate Democrat said that he is against sending more U.S. combat troops to Afghanistan until the training and equipping of Afghan security forces can be expanded.

2009-09-11.title: 2 Say They Reported Abuses at Embassy
2009-09-11.abstract: Former employees of the security contractor ArmorGroup charged that State Department officials were aware of problems in the United States Embassy in Kabul as early as 2007.

2009-09-11.title: Arctic Shortcut Beckons Shippers as Ice Thaws
2009-09-11.abstract: Two German ships are poised to complete, for the first time, a commercial shipment transit from Asia to Europe over the waters of the Arctic north of Russia.

2009-09-11.title: Turkey: Kurdish Studies Approved
2009-09-11.abstract: This year, for the first time, Turkey’s ethnic Kurds will be able to study their language at a Turkish university.

2009-09-12.title: On Web, Storeroom Crooner From Tajikistan Is a Star
2009-09-12.abstract: The rise of Baimurat Allaberiyev, a migrant worker in Russia who delivers astonishing renditions of Bollywood musical numbers, is a testament to the strange power of the Internet.

2009-09-12.title: France: Critics Ask Official to Quit
2009-09-12.abstract: Interior Minister Brice Hortefeux faced calls for his resignation on Friday after a video surfaced in which he made comments that critics said were anti-Arab.

2009-09-12.title: Russia: Deciding Candidacy Jointly
2009-09-12.abstract: Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin said that he and his protégé, President Dmitri A. Medvedev, would decide together who would run for president from their party in 2012.

2009-09-12.title: For a Bounced Check in Dubai, the Penalty Can Be Years Behind Bars
2009-09-12.abstract: The criminalization of debt has put a formidable weapon in the hands of landlords, banks and other creditors, who can send someone to jail with a single document showing a check has been returned.

2009-09-12.title: U.S. to Accept Iran’s Proposal to Hold Face-to-Face Talks
2009-09-12.abstract: Engaging with Iran would put a senior representative of the Obama administration at the bargaining table, along with emissaries from five other nations.

2009-09-13.title: U.S. to Expand Detainee Review in Afghan Prison
2009-09-13.abstract: The Obama administration plans to issue new guidelines aimed at giving the hundreds of prisoners at Bagram Air Base more ability to challenge their custody, officials say.

2009-09-13.title: Barrichello Leads Brawn to Another Double Victory
2009-09-13.abstract: Rubens Barrichello leads Brawn to another victory.

2009-09-13.title: Rubens and His Teammates
2009-09-13.abstract: Rubens Barichello talks about racing his teammate, Jenson Button, for the F1 title.

2009-09-13.title: Europeans to Keep Sanctions on Zimbabwe
2009-09-13.abstract: A delegation criticized President Robert Mugabe for continuing to block the replacement of the attorney general, regional governors and central bank chief.

2009-09-13.title: Norway Votes as Coalition Finds Itself on Defensive
2009-09-13.abstract: Norway’s left-leaning prime minister, Jens Stoltenberg, has asked voters to renew their commitment to one of the world’s most advanced social welfare models.

2009-09-14.title: Fervent Believers
2009-09-14.abstract: Some Israeli settlers believe that there is a divine plan requiring them to hold the land, many of them armed. They live by the slogan “Never forget! Never forgive!”

2009-09-14.title: Prosecutors in Iraq Case See Pattern by Guards
2009-09-14.abstract: Prosecutors charge Blackwater guards repeatedly shot into the streets of Baghdad without regard for civilians.

2009-09-14.title: Iran Opposition Leader’s Aide Is Freed
2009-09-14.abstract: The release of the aide, Alireza Hosseini-Beheshti, appears to be a sign of retreat by the hard-core conservative authorities running Iran.

2009-09-14.title: Keeping the Faith in Homesh
2009-09-14.abstract: The Times's Ethan Bronner looks at the state of settlements on the West Bank as further pressure is applied to remove them.

2009-09-14.title: Resolve of West Bank Settlers May Have Limits
2009-09-14.abstract: Ideological settlers are unlikely to engage in organized armed conflict with the Israeli military, as some fear, because their beliefs make them hesitant to turn against their own.

2009-09-15.title: German Candidates’ Debate Hits Soft Notes
2009-09-15.abstract: The debate between Chancellor Angela Merkel and Foreign Minister Frank-Walter Steinmeier was billed as a duel, but it will go down in history as “the duet.”

2009-09-15.title: Underneath Palatial Skin, Corruption Rules Gabon
2009-09-15.abstract: Behind the late ruler’s palaces, which line wide empty boulevards, are shacks and shanties stretching to the horizon.

2009-09-15.title: Fight Nights and Reggae Pack Brazilian Churches
2009-09-15.abstract: A growing evangelical movement in Brazil is attracting young people by adopting their culture.

2009-09-15.title: South Africa: Hot Line Takes First Calls
2009-09-15.abstract: Fulfilling a campaign promise, President Jacob Zuma started a hot line for citizen complaints on Monday.

2009-09-15.title: Pakistan Army Said to Be Linked to Swat Killings
2009-09-15.abstract: Two months after the Pakistani Army gained control of the Swat Valley, bodies have been dumped on the streets in what rights advocates and residents say is the work of the military.

2009-09-16.title: The Camel as Cow, a Cautionary Tale
2009-09-16.abstract: A Dutch farmer sees an untapped market among immigrants from Somalia and Morocco, where camel’s milk has long been popular.

2009-09-16.title: Clinton Lays Out Iran Requirements
2009-09-16.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton said the Obama administration would not be drawn into lengthy and worthless talks.

2009-09-16.title: Breaking in New Sport, Dutch Sweat Small Stuff
2009-09-16.abstract: Lock picking for sport, which sprang up over the last decade in the Netherlands and northern Germany, grew out of computer hacking.

2009-09-16.title: Indian Women Find New Peace in Rail Commute
2009-09-16.abstract: Eight new commuter trains exclusively for women have been introduced in four Indian cities.

2009-09-16.title: Zimbabwe: Help for Schoolchildren
2009-09-16.abstract: Donors will provide $70 million to pay the school fees for 700,000 poor children and to buy more than seven million textbooks for children in Zimbabwe.

2009-09-17.title: A Scandal Over Spying Intensifies in Colombia
2009-09-17.abstract: The Department of Administrative Security has been accused of illegally spying on President Álvaro Uribe’s critics.

2009-09-17.title: A ‘French Chef’ Whose Appeal Doesn’t Translate
2009-09-17.abstract: Julia Child may have been America’s best-known “French chef,” but in Paris few know her fabled cookbooks, let alone her name.

2009-09-17.title: Indian Women Escape Harassment on the Train
2009-09-17.abstract: In a pilot program, eight new commuter trains exclusively for female passengers have been introduced in India’s four largest cities.

2009-09-17.title: A Talk With Somalia’s President
2009-09-17.abstract: Excerpts from a transcript of Jeffrey Gettleman’s recent interview with Somalia’s president, Sheik Sharif Sheik Ahmed.

2009-09-17.title: In Somalia, a Leader Is Raising Hopes for Stability
2009-09-17.abstract: Analysts say President Sheik Sharif Sheik Ahmed offers hope for stability, but he needs to act against militants.

2009-09-18.title: A City Reinvents Itself Beyond Conflict
2009-09-18.abstract: As it celebrates its 100th anniversary, Tel Aviv tends to be engaged in a debate not so much about its tumultuous past as about its future.

2009-09-18.title: In a New Age, Bahrain Struggles to Honor the Dead While Serving the Living
2009-09-18.abstract: The destruction of ancient burial sites illustrates conflicting priorities and the divide between the elite and the disenfranchised.

2009-09-18.title: Afghan Vote Uncertainty Sparks Dilemma for U.S.
2009-09-18.abstract: The White House fears the election may not produce a clear winner until spring, leaving Afghanistan without a credible leader for months.

2009-09-18.abstract: Secretary General Ban Ki-moon will address the plight of the world’s poor, along with other issues, at a coming United Nations meeting in New York.

2009-09-18.abstract: While President Obama’s missile defense plan relies on scaled-back technology, it remains, like Ronald Reagan’s vision of a Star Wars system, grounded in international politics.

2009-09-19.title: C.I.A. Chiefs Ask Obama to Stop Abuse Inquiry
2009-09-19.abstract: Former intelligence chiefs say pursuing an investigation into abuses during interrogations could harm national security.

2009-09-19.title: Hunger Strikers Press for Iraq’s Release of Iranian Exiles
2009-09-19.abstract: The protesters, who are encamped near the White House, want the Obama administration’s support for their cause. Similar strikes are under way around the world.

2009-09-19.title: Convicted Lockerbie Bomber Posts Documents
2009-09-19.abstract: Abdel Basset Ali al-Megrahi, the Libyan recently sent home by Scottish authorities, offered what he said was evidence to support his claim of innocence.

2009-09-19.title: Lack of Progress in Mideast Defies Obama’s Hopes
2009-09-19.abstract: After a fruitless week of diplomacy, President Obama is unlikely to be able to announce plans to restart talks.

2009-09-19.title: U.N. Study Is Called Unfair to Israel
2009-09-19.abstract: The report, issued by a commission led by a South African judge, said Israel had used disproportionate force in Gaza, resulting in the death of about 1,400 civilians.

2009-09-20.title: 5 Killed in Suicide Bombing
2009-09-20.abstract: A suicide bomber killed 5 people and wounded 16 others in the southern city of Kandahar on Saturday evening, as the Muslim holy month of Ramadan drew to a close.

2009-09-20.title: Obama to Meet With Mideast Leaders
2009-09-20.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian leaders will make their cases that the Obama administration should push the other harder.

2009-09-20.title: Copter Crash Kills U.S. Soldier in Iraq
2009-09-20.abstract: An American helicopter crashed at an airbase north of Baghdad, killing one soldier and wounding 12 others, the American military said.

2009-09-20.title: Pakistan Says Top Commander With Taliban Died in Custody
2009-09-20.abstract: Sher Mohammad Qasab was arrested along with one of his sons on Sept. 16. He was one of the top 21 Taliban commanders in Swat.

2009-09-20.title: Obama Won’t Say if More Troops to Be Sent to Afghanistan
2009-09-20.abstract: With the administration reviewing its military strategy, President Obama would not tip his hand on whether he would approve sending more troops to Afghanistan.

2009-09-21.title: Party’s Agenda in China Seems to Fall Flat
2009-09-21.abstract: Initial reports from the Communist Party’s Central Committee policy meetings suggested that there were few, if any, changes.

2009-09-21.title: One in 4 Afghan Ballots Face Check for Fraud
2009-09-21.abstract: Thousands of polling stations are subject to a fraud review, according to an analysis by The New York Times.

2009-09-21.title: General Calls for More U.S. Troops to Avoid Afghan Failure
2009-09-21.abstract: Gen. Stanley A. McChrystal warned in a confidential assessment that he needed additional troops by next year or the conflict “will likely result in failure.”

2009-09-21.title: Setting the Stage for the Recount
2009-09-21.abstract: An analysis of the Afghan presidential vote.

2009-09-21.title: Pakistan Places Mumbai Suspect Under House Arrest
2009-09-21.abstract: It was the first legal move against Hafiz Muhammad Saeed, whose Muslim charity fronts for the banned militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba.

2009-09-22.title: Taiwan: City to Show Uighur Film
2009-09-22.abstract: Government officials in Kaohsiung have refused to back down from their decision to show a documentary about Rebiya Kadeer, the exiled Uighur leader.

2009-09-22.title: Philippines: Rebel Camp Overrun
2009-09-22.abstract: The Philippine military on Monday overran a camp in the south belonging to the terrorist group Abu Sayyaf, killing nearly 20 militants, the authorities said.

2009-09-22.title: France: Villepin Trial Opens
2009-09-22.abstract: On the first day of his civil trial in the so-called Clearstream affair, former Prime Minister Dominique de Villepin accused President Nicolas Sarkozy of pursuing him for political reasons.

2009-09-22.title: Somalia: Shabab Extends Reach
2009-09-22.abstract: The Shabab insurgents have taken over Yeed, a town near the Ethiopian border in the Bakol region.

2009-09-22.title: China: Swine Flu Campaign, First in World, Begins in Beijing
2009-09-22.abstract: On Monday, China became the first country in the world to begin a national swine flu vaccination campaign.

2009-09-23.title: Mixed Verdict in S. African Lesbian’s Murder Trial
2009-09-23.abstract: The sexual orientation of Eudy Simelane, a lesbian activist and soccer player, was never acknowledged as being a factor in her murder.

2009-09-23.title: Aristocrat’s Rise Shakes German Doldrums
2009-09-23.abstract: The German economics minister, Karl-Theodor zu Guttenberg, is known more for his appearances in the tabloids than his growing list of achievements.

2009-09-23.title: U.N. Sets an Example by Offsetting Its Carbon Emissions
2009-09-23.abstract: A conference that generated the equivalent of 461 tons of carbon dioxide will end up helping a rural power project in India.

2009-09-23.abstract: American officials have been wary of driving Afghans “into the hands of the Taliban” while they discourage poppy growing.

2009-09-23.title: Travelers’ Fee Can Help Fight Diseases
2009-09-23.abstract: The extension of a United Nations program is expected to add hundreds of millions of dollars to a health program.

2009-09-24.title: Greece: Lawmaker’s Home Hit
2009-09-24.abstract: The Athens office and residence of a senior Greek Socialist lawmaker, Louka Katseli, was bombed on Wednesday, ahead of elections on Oct. 4.

2009-09-24.title: Al Qaeda Releases Another Video
2009-09-24.abstract: Ayman al-Zawahri, the No. 2 leader of Al Qaeda and other members of the terrorist group appeared in a new video to mark the eighth anniversary of the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.

2009-09-24.title: China: 11 Indicted in Factory Brawl
2009-09-24.abstract: Prosecutors in southern China have indicted 11 people who they say took part in a factory brawl in Guangdong Province .

2009-09-24.title: European Patrols Brace for Rise in Tension Over Report on 2008 War in Georgia
2009-09-24.abstract: There has been heated speculation about the European Union report, which will be presented Wednesday to the organization’s Council of Ministers.

2009-09-24.title: Iraq: Attacks Kill at Least 6 People
2009-09-24.abstract: A series of roadside bombs and shootings in northern and western Iraq on Wednesday killed at least six people, four of them security officers.

2009-09-25.title: American Helped Bomb Somalia Base, Web Site Says
2009-09-25.abstract: The F.B.I. said about 20 Somali-Americans previously joined a terrorist group in Somalia.

2009-09-25.title: Mexican Is Confirmed as Attorney General
2009-09-25.abstract: The Senate confirmed Arturo Chávez, following questioning of his performance investigating murders in Ciudad Juárez as a state prosecutor in the 1990s.

2009-09-25.title: Kenya: U.S. Pressure for Reforms
2009-09-25.abstract: The United States threatened Thursday to impose travel bans on top Kenyan politicians if they did not move faster to effect reforms.

2009-09-25.title: Threat to the Mekong Delta
2009-09-25.abstract: Climate change could bring longer-lasting disruptions to millions of people living in Vietnam's Mekong Delta than generations of warfare.

2009-09-25.title: Report on Security Council
2009-09-25.abstract: Neal MacFarquhar, the United Nations bureau chief for The New York Times, talks about Thursday's special Security Council session on nuclear arms presided over by President Obama.

2009-09-26.title: Venezuela Says Iran Is Helping It Look for Uranium
2009-09-26.abstract: The disclosure points to the importance President Hugo Chávez has placed on the development of a nuclear energy program.

2009-09-26.title: Cryptic Note Ignited an Iran Nuclear Strategy Debate
2009-09-26.abstract: The Obama administration seized upon Iran’s disclosure of a nuclear site to marshal its own evidence and try to persuade wavering nations to take a hard line with the country.

2009-09-26.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran made light of the Western accusation that his country was caught hiding another crucial piece of its nuclear development program.

2009-09-26.title: Israeli Ambassador Draws on American Roots
2009-09-26.abstract: For Michael B. Oren, the hardest thing about becoming Israel’s ambassador to the United States was giving up his American citizenship.

2009-09-26.title: Corrections
2009-09-26.abstract: An article on Thursday about the United Nations General Assembly speech by Col. Muammar al-Qaddafi, the Libyan leader, misidentified, in some copies, the Westchester County suburb where local authorities had blocked an effort by Libyan diplomats to pitch his reception tent. It is Bedford, not Brewster. The article also misspelled the surname of the General Assembly president, who is a former Libyan foreign minister and United Nations representative. It is Ali Treki, not Treiki. (The spelling was officially changed from Treiki to Treki when he was elected to the post on June 10.).

2009-09-27.title: Deadly Bomb Targets Afghan Minister
2009-09-27.abstract: A car bomb narrowly missed Afghanistan’s energy minister as he was driving through the city of Herat.

2009-09-27.title: Merkel’s Party Claims Victory in Germany
2009-09-27.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel’s conservative party was projected to form a new center-right government, ending its coalition with the Social Democrats.

2009-09-27.title: Hamilton Wins a Wild Singapore Grand Prix
2009-09-27.abstract: Lewis Hamilton won the Singapore Grand Prix ahead of Timo Glock in a Toyota and Fernando Alonso in a Renault.

2009-09-27.title: Wartime Soldier, Conflicted Mom
2009-09-27.abstract: Lizette Alvarez reports on the unique challenges faced by mothers in the military in the latest article in the Women at Arms series.

2009-09-27.title: Bits ‘n Pieces From Singapore Race
2009-09-27.abstract: Roundup of events at the Singapore Grand Prix.

2009-09-28.title: For Brazil, Olympic Bid Is About Global Role
2009-09-28.abstract: Winning the Games would be a transformational moment for Brazil, an affirmation of its rising global importance.

2009-09-28.title: Death Toll Expected to Rise After Storm in the Philippines
2009-09-28.abstract: Countless people remained trapped on their roofs on Monday because of floodwaters in the aftermath of Tropical Storm Ketsana.

2009-09-28.title: India and Pakistan Fail to Restart Negotiations
2009-09-28.abstract: After meeting on the sidelines of the United Nations General Assembly, both sides were stuck again on the fallout from the Mumbai attack.

2009-09-28.title: Honduras Bars Diplomats as Political Crisis Grows
2009-09-28.abstract: The increasingly isolated de facto government also threatened to shut down the Brazilian Embassy where the ousted president has been holed up for a week.

2009-09-28.title: Many Investors Still Avoid Risks of Iraq
2009-09-28.abstract: The experience of one Iraqi company shows that economic development and foreign investment face more obstacles than security alone.

2009-09-29.title: Egypt Ponders Failed Drive for Unesco
2009-09-29.abstract: Many Egyptians said that Farouk Hosny’s failure to lead Unesco was at least as much a sign of Egypt’s long, slow slide as the center of Arab culture, thought and influence.

2009-09-29.title: China Prepares for National Day Celebration
2009-09-29.abstract: The country will observe the 60th anniversary of the People’s Republic of China with a parade that will make 76 trombones look like a child’s plastic kazoo.

2009-09-29.title: A Nuclear Debate: Is Iran Designing Warheads?
2009-09-29.abstract: A dispute among American, European and Israeli spies about Iran’s advanced weapons work has huge political consequences.

2009-09-29.title: Iran: The Nuclear Question
2009-09-29.abstract: The Times's David E. Sanger discusses the issues surrounding the discovery of the Iranian enrichment site near the holy city of Qum.

2009-09-29.title: Iran’s Hidden Nuclear Facility
2009-09-29.abstract: Images show the details of a hidden facility in Iran that experts say is the nuclear site recently disclosed by the Obama administration. Information about the plant is from an analysis by IHS Jane’s.

2009-09-30.title: From Turbines and Straw, Danish Self-Sufficiency
2009-09-30.abstract: A tiny island just completed a 10-year experiment to see whether it could become energy self-sufficient, and its residents say they have met the goal.

2009-09-30.title: American Samoa: 14 Die as Quake Sets Off a Tsunami
2009-09-30.abstract: Towering tsunami waves created by a powerful earthquake with a magnitude between 8.0 and 8.3 swept ashore on two South Pacific island chains.

2009-09-30.title: General Says Iraq Troop Reductions May Quicken
2009-09-30.abstract: A faster drawdown could ease the strain across the armed forces and free up extra units for duty in Afghanistan.

2009-09-30.title: Dozens Die as Tsunami Hits Samoa Islands
2009-09-30.abstract: An 8.0-magnitude earthquake sent waves over the Samoa islands, reportedly killing at least several dozen people.

2009-09-30.title: In Mexico City, a Political Deal Redone
2009-09-30.abstract: After winning a municipal post in Mexico City with the help of a backroom deal, an activist-turned-politician agrees to give it up — on his terms.
